description_long: This marble sculpture depicts two classical figures standing side by side on a rectangular plinth. The figure on the left is a nude male, muscular and youthful, with one hand raised to his chest and the other arm extended, possibly in a gesture of conversation or supplication. The figure on the right is a robed female, wearing a helmet and holding a staff or spear, with an owl perched on her left arm, suggesting she represents Athena, the Greek goddess of wisdom and war. The drapery of the female figure is intricately carved, with deep folds and realistic texture, while the male figure's anatomy is rendered with careful attention to musculature. Both figures are life-sized and stand in a dynamic, interactive pose, with the female figure slightly behind and to the side of the male. The sculpture is rendered in a white or light gray stone, likely marble, and sits on a solid rectangular base.
description: A life-sized marble sculpture of a nude male and a robed, helmeted female (likely Athena) standing together on a rectangular plinth. The figures are intricately detailed, with realistic anatomy and drapery.
description_view_0: Front view showing both figures in full detail, with the male figure's chest and the female figure's helmet, staff, and owl clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view from the left, highlighting the depth of the drapery on the female figure and the profile of both figures.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the back of the female figure's robe and helmet, as well as the male figure's back and the owl's position.
description_view_3: Angled rear-right view, emphasizing the musculature of the male figure's back and the layered folds of the female figure's robe.
attribute_subject_matter: Classical figures, likely Athena and a male youth, in a mythological scene
attribute_location_context: No visible context; the sculpture is isolated on a plain background with no environmental cues.
